rows.objection: Posterior masks only need to beat random masks.
rows.reviewer_risk: The positive posterior signal could be overstated if random is the only baseline.
rows.current_answer: Posterior supports usually beat random, but this signal disappears against chain-start and rewind magnitude controls.
rows.key_numbers: posterior>random 58/59 groups; posterior>chain by >0.005 0/59; rewind>posterior by >0.005 55/57.
rows.remaining_gap: None for the support-equivalence claim; this remains a negative result.

rows.objection: The posterior sampler is too weak or stuck in one chain.
rows.reviewer_risk: A better-mixed sampler might reveal ticket-like posterior supports.
rows.current_answer: Dense-start and independent-start multi-chain cyclical SGLD increase posterior sample count and chain diversity without recovering mask-distribution equivalence.
rows.key_numbers: full SGLD p=5.34e-09, hamming=0.0033; dense-start cSGLD samples=75, p=3.33e-08, hamming=0.2461; independent-start cSGLD samples=75, p=9.30e-10, hamming=0.0000.
rows.remaining_gap: Still open for a qualitatively different exact full-network CIFAR posterior.

rows.objection: Function-space agreement is enough even if masks differ.
rows.reviewer_risk: High CKA could make support mismatch look like a parameterization artifact.
rows.current_answer: Function-space CKA is high, but the proposal requires mask-distribution equivalence; layer sparsity, Hamming overlap, and basin counts fail.
rows.key_numbers: full SGLD logit/activation CKA=0.9369/0.9172, hamming=0.0033; JointDiag270k CKA=0.9373/0.9199, p=1.09e-08; LowRank128 hamming=0.8163 but p=1.98e-06.
evidence: paper/main.tex: direct proposal-level CIFAR mode/ticket paragraphs
rows.remaining_gap: A paper revision should keep this distinction explicit in the main text.

rows.objection: A channel permutation or re-basin step would align posterior masks.
rows.reviewer_risk: Naive parameter coordinates may compare different neuron orderings.
rows.current_answer: Activation/weight alignment, record-level matching, and a global block-coordinate channel audit all remain negative; exact full-data graph isomorphism is quantified as infeasible.
rows.key_numbers: global-channel posterior/ticket Hamming=0.2105; support overlap=0.3738; exact stage-1 assignments=128; full search log10 assignments=840.4.
rows.remaining_gap: Exhaustive full-data graph isomorphism remains unimplemented and infeasible.

rows.objection: The covariance posterior is too diagonal, local, or head-only.
rows.reviewer_risk: A richer posterior covariance could be the missing ticket signal.
rows.current_answer: The covariance-fidelity trend audit spans full-network rank-16/32/64/128 Hessian-plus-diagonal rows, exact tensor-block and joint-group rows up to the 270,896-weight vector, direct LowRank128 and JointDiag270k distribution rows, and exact dense small-model code-path checks; literal dense CIFAR covariance is outside the workstation budget.
rows.key_numbers: movement/direct covariance rows=9/2; max movement posterior-chain=0.0036; min exact rewind-posterior=0.0292; JointDiag270k samples=25, p=1.09e-08, hamming=0.0000; dense CIFAR matrix=553.1 GiB; matrix+Cholesky=1,106.3 GiB.
rows.remaining_gap: Exact dense full-network CIFAR posterior evidence is still absent.

rows.objection: Linear mode connectivity explains the ticket/posterior relation.
rows.reviewer_risk: Low-loss paths might make posterior modes and tickets equivalent after all.
rows.current_answer: Barrier size and support equivalence are empirically orthogonal in the current artifacts.
rows.key_numbers: MNIST/Fashion dense-IMP barriers=0.0026/0.0395; CIFAR long SGLD/SWAG barriers=3.0827/3.7402; posterior-chain gaps stay <=0.001.
rows.remaining_gap: Nonlinear re-basin paths remain conceptually distinct from the tested linear barriers.

rows.objection: Learned Bayesian or variational masks could recover tickets.
rows.reviewer_risk: Support failures might be specific to sample-magnitude maps.
rows.current_answer: Gem-Miner-style, Bernoulli/Concrete variational, and hard-concrete mask sources are below IMP in current CIFAR support/accuracy and do not rescue calibration/OOD tradeoffs.
rows.key_numbers: IMP acc/AUROC=0.8953/0.8306; SWAG acc/AUROC=0.8688/0.8050; variational acc/AUROC=0.8301/0.7754; variational/hard-concrete support=0.0907/0.0922.
rows.remaining_gap: Broader permutation-aware learned-mask distributions remain optional hardening.

rows.objection: A simpler trajectory or process subspace explains the IMP residual.
rows.reviewer_risk: The alternative mechanism could be an artifact of coarse controls.
rows.current_answer: Tensor+score matching and learned-subspace residualization reduce but do not replace the process-selected IMP residual coordinates.
rows.key_numbers: tensor+score round/replacement acc=0.8878/0.8837, delta=0.0041; learned-subspace round/residualized acc=0.8869/0.8821, delta=0.0048.
rows.remaining_gap: Additional causal process interventions would strengthen the positive mechanism.

rows.objection: The artifact is venue-submission ready, but strict external GPU hardening is pending.
rows.reviewer_risk: Even strong evidence can fail review if reproducibility and paper packaging are weak.
rows.current_answer: Local checks, main-only submission PDF, CPU artifact verification, release anonymization, local archive build/extraction smoke, and source-only repository snapshot smoke pass. The venue compliance audit marks the manuscript packet and local release packaging ready. Strict external validation is intentionally not claimed: public release upload, public repository state, external CI, and external CUDA-host GPU-container receipts remain unobserved for the current archive/source snapshot.
rows.key_numbers: latest local `make check` and `scripts/verify_research_artifacts.py` passed; main-only PDF is 9 pages; local release archive and source snapshot smoke pass; strict external-validation gate remains not ready with `public_release_upload_not_verified`, `public_repository_state_not_verified`, `external_ci_run_not_observed`, and `external_gpu_container_run_not_observed`.
rows.remaining_gap: Publish the current archive/source snapshot and collect public release, public repository, external CI, and external CUDA-host GPU-container receipts before claiming strict external validation.
